claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for helping me with this. I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my child Alex, who is 9 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's lovely to meet you, and I'm happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Now, could you tell me: do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you! 

Next, could you tell me about your living arrangements with Alex? Does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you for confirming that.

Just a couple more questions to ensure eligibility:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One more question: Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation, or have they been in any of these for an extended period?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital, care, or residential accommodation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for all that information. Based on what you've told me, I have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

- ✅ You live in the UK
- ✅ Alex is under 16 years old (aged 9)
- ✅ Alex lives with you, meaning you're responsible for them
- ✅ Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation
- ✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit. Please note that only one person can receive Child Benefit for a child, so if anyone else becomes responsible for Alex in the future, you would need to agree between yourselves who should claim.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old, which is under 16, so they meet the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them, meaning the claimant is responsible for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for an extended period. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for one child, Alex (aged 9), who lives with them. Alex is under 16, not in hospital or care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 9 years old, which is under 16, so they meet the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them, meaning the claimant is responsible for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for an extended period. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for one child, Alex (aged 9), who lives with them. Alex is under 16, not in hospital or care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
